seed: control vs surrender; overflow_vs_containment; overfilled_center; stutter_step; hinge against negative_cross; thick_outline; pressure_vs_release; compass; lonely_signal | abstract variation request 98; target batch count 500
title: Tension in Transition
reasoning: This artwork explores the emotional dichotomy of control and surrender. It features muted colors from the 'broken_memory' palette, suggesting a nostalgic weight, with sepia and faded blue offering a calm yet complex background. The primary contrast emerges at the center, where lighter colors 'overflow' against darker, more contained tones, resembling the struggle between a chaotic push and a stabilizing force. Patterns of dots represent control, while gradient steps reflect the slow, relinquished descent of surrender. Broken pixels are interspersed to symbolize moments of imperfection and the emotional scars from this tension. This composition illustrates a quiet battle—defining how contrasts collide and negotiate within the confines of the square, alluding to personal struggles devoid of literal imagery.

layout.name: mirror_broken
layout.description: Left and right sides almost mirror, but 2 to 5 pixels are wrong.

composition.name: light_vs_weight
composition.idea: A small bright shape fights against a heavy dark mass.

referenceExample.title: Awe at the Edge
referenceExample.pattern: black hollow center surrounded by cold gold and star-white pressure

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
